items.title: Reentrancy in claim() allows draining rewards
items.body: The claim function makes an external call to msg.sender before updating the user's claimed mapping, allowing a reentrant call to claim multiple times. Root cause: external call precedes state write (CEI violation).
items.recommendation: Apply checks-effects-interactions or a reentrancy guard.
items.code: function claim() external { uint256 amt = rewards[msg.sender]; (bool ok,) = msg.sender.call{value: amt}(""); rewards[msg.sender] = 0; }

items.title: Use of tx.origin for authorization
items.body: The onlyOwner check uses tx.origin == owner, which can be phished via an intermediary contract.

items.title: Gas optimization in loop
items.body: Cache array length outside the loop to save gas.

items.title: Missing event on critical parameter change
items.body: setFee does not emit an event.
items.code: function setFee(uint f) external onlyOwner { fee = f; }
